summary: Reviewed the live-event reliability pack for unhappy-path workflow failures around admin mutations, realtime/polling recovery, migrations, and jackpot handling. The main risks are non-atomic check-then-write/delete flows, partial destructive resets, and connection-health logic that can misclassify live display state. Overall, the happy-path protections are stronger than before, but several failure windows can still break a live night if admin and host actions overlap or if Supabase calls fail mid-flow.
appears_sound: Prize completeness is enforced server-side in both `createGame` and `updateGame`, with whitespace trimmed before persistence in `src/app/admin/sessions/[id]/actions.ts:81`.

`state_version` is added to both private and public game-state rows, copied through `sync_game_states_public()`, and selected explicitly by display/player pages in `supabase/migrations/20260430120000_add_state_version.sql:1`.
The proxy matcher remains tightly scoped to `/admin/:path*`, `/host/:path*`, and `/login`, preserving public display/player performance in `src/proxy.ts:7`.
Invite-only login is preserved: signup returns a hard failure and `next` is sanitized against absolute/protocol-relative redirects in `src/app/login/actions.ts:6`.

Delete flows now check started game states and recorded winners before deletion, improving the ordinary-path guardrails in `src/app/admin/actions.ts:116` and `src/app/admin/sessions/[id]/actions.ts:310`.

findings.description: Failure sequence: admin opens a started game to edit only name/order/notes, the locked structural fieldset is disabled, the browser omits `type`, `stages`, `snowball_pot_id`, and prize inputs from `FormData`, and `updateGame` compares null/empty values against the original locked fields. The save is rejected even though the UI says only prize/type/snowball/stage configuration is locked.
findings.evidence: The modal disables the whole structural fieldset at `src/app/admin/sessions/[id]/session-detail.tsx:552`, while `updateGame` reads those disabled fields from `formData` and lock-compares them at `src/app/admin/sessions/[id]/actions.ts:160`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If the intended policy is to block all edits after start, the UI copy and server allowlist should be changed to say that explicitly.
findings.what_would_confirm: Submit a started game with unchanged structural fields and only a notes/name change, then inspect whether the server returns the locked-fields error.

findings.description: Failure sequence: admin submits a structural/prize edit while the host starts the same game after the status read but before the game update; the action still writes type/prizes/stage changes after the game has become live. This violates the per-game lock in the exact race the lock is meant to protect.
findings.evidence: `updateGame` reads `game_states.status` first at `src/app/admin/sessions/[id]/actions.ts:145`, then later performs an unconditional `.from('games').update(...)` without a transactional or conditional status guard at `src/app/admin/sessions/[id]/actions.ts:224`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The race requires admin and host actions to overlap on the same game, so it may be rare operationally.
findings.what_would_confirm: Run concurrent start-game and update-game requests and verify whether a structural update can commit after `game_states.status` becomes `in_progress`.

findings.description: Failure sequence: delete-session checks pass, then a host starts a game or records a winner before the final delete; the delete still targets only `sessions.id` and can remove a now-live session/game graph. The same check-then-delete window exists for per-game deletion.
findings.evidence: `deleteSession` performs started/winner checks before an unconditional `.from('sessions').delete().eq('id', sessionId)` at `src/app/admin/actions.ts:163`; `deleteGame` follows the same precheck pattern in `src/app/admin/sessions/[id]/actions.ts:310`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: Staff may avoid deleting sessions during a live event, but the code does not enforce that workflow boundary.
findings.what_would_confirm: Exercise a concurrent delete and host-start/winner-insert flow, or replace the sequence with a transaction/RPC that locks and deletes atomically.

findings.description: Failure sequence: reset starts, deletes `game_states`, then a later winner delete or status update fails; the session can remain running/partially reset with no live game state for host/display recovery. This is a destructive multi-step workflow without a transaction or compensating rollback.
findings.evidence: `resetSession` deletes `game_states` at `src/app/admin/sessions/[id]/actions.ts:405` and then performs separate later cleanup calls such as winner deletion at `src/app/admin/sessions/[id]/actions.ts:417`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: Retrying reset may eventually complete, but the intermediate state is still broken during the failure window.
findings.what_would_confirm: Inject a failure after the game-state delete and verify the persisted session, active game, winners, and display behavior.

findings.description: Failure sequence: polling fails while realtime payloads still arrive, but `poll-failure` marks the page unhealthy and realtime payloads do not clear it, so the banner/auto-refresh can interrupt a working live display. Conversely, a bare `SUBSCRIBED` status marks the page healthy even if no fresh game-state payload has arrived and polling is still failing.
findings.evidence: `poll-failure` unconditionally flips unhealthy at `src/lib/connection-health.ts:44`, while `SUBSCRIBED` flips healthy at `src/lib/connection-health.ts:52` without requiring a successful data update.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If polling failures are very rare and short-lived, operators may not notice the false banner/refresh behavior.
findings.what_would_confirm: Simulate continuous poll failures with realtime payloads still applying, and simulate `SUBSCRIBED` with no payload plus failed polls.

findings.description: Failure sequence: session active game changes from A to B while the fetch for A is slow; if A resolves after B, it can overwrite `currentActiveGame` and `currentGameState`, leaving display/player on the wrong game until another update. Polling has sequence guards, but active-game refresh does not.
findings.evidence: `refreshActiveGame` awaits game and game-state fetches and then calls setters with no request sequence or active id recheck in `src/app/display/[sessionId]/display-ui.tsx:88`; the same pattern appears in `src/app/player/[sessionId]/player-ui.tsx:95`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: Active-game changes are usually human-paced, so out-of-order responses may be uncommon.
findings.what_would_confirm: Mock delayed Supabase responses for two rapid active_game_id changes and assert the later id always wins.

findings.description: Failure sequence: after a snowball winner flow, a transient failure reading `games` or `snowball_pots` can be treated as success, so the jackpot pot is not reset/rolled over and the host may see no error. This undermines the cross-session jackpot workflow after a live result.
findings.evidence: `handleSnowballPotUpdate` ignores the `games` query error and returns success when `gameData` is missing at `src/app/host/actions.ts:113`, and also returns success when `potData` is missing at `src/app/host/actions.ts:138`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The visible pack truncates the `recordWinner` caller, so this depends on that helper being used in the winner completion path.
findings.what_would_confirm: Inspect or test `recordWinner`/game-completion paths to confirm `handleSnowballPotUpdate` is invoked and that read failures currently surface as success.

findings.description: Failure sequence: an existing nullable `call_delay_seconds` row remains `NULL` because SQL `<> 2` does not match nulls, so host/display timing can bypass the intended two-second gap for that row. The schema does not declare the column `not null`, so this migration does not fully satisfy its own backfill comment.
findings.evidence: The migration uses `where call_delay_seconds <> 2` for both tables at `supabase/migrations/20260430120200_backfill_call_delay_to_2.sql:9`, which excludes `NULL` values in PostgreSQL.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If production has no null `call_delay_seconds` rows, this has no immediate data impact.
findings.what_would_confirm: Query production for `call_delay_seconds is null` or change the backfill predicate to `call_delay_seconds is distinct from 2`.

empty_categories: No proxy matcher safety issue found; public `/display/*`, `/player/*`, and `/` routes are not added to middleware scope.
No new winner uniqueness constraint appears in the shown migrations, so multi-winner support is not contradicted by this pack.
No login open-redirect failure found in the changed login action; non-relative and protocol-relative `next` values are sanitized.
No Date.now impurity found in the pure connection-health reducer/selectors; wall-clock reads are kept in the hook layer.
